mcqs.question: What is the primary function of a MAC protocol?

options.text: To manage access to a shared communication medium
To perform error detection and correction
To regulate the amount of data that can be transmitted at one time
To identify devices on a network

mcqs.explanation: MAC protocols manage access to a shared communication medium, ensuring that only one device can transmit data at a time.

mcqs.question: Which MAC protocol is used in Ethernet networks?

options.text: CSMA/CD
Token Ring
ALOHA
CSMA

mcqs.explanation: CSMA/CD is a random access protocol used in Ethernet networks.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of controlled access protocols?

options.text: They are simple to implement
They have low latency
They prevent collisions
They are easy to manage in large networks

mcqs.explanation: Controlled access protocols prevent collisions by taking turns transmitting data.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of random access protocols?

options.text: They are complex to implement
They have high latency
They can introduce collisions
They are difficult to manage in large networks

mcqs.explanation: Random access protocols can introduce collisions, which can lead to data loss and retransmission.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of framing in MAC protocols?

options.text: To add headers and trailers to the data
To perform error detection and correction
To regulate the amount of data that can be transmitted at one time
To identify devices on a network

mcqs.explanation: Framing adds headers and trailers to the data, allowing devices to identify the start and end of a frame.
